html:
[5856]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 有声役寝无音响恐惧既甚亦不敢寻究 - Describes a situation where the person hears sounds but does not hear any clear noise, causing great fear and preventing them from investigating further.
  • 但把劔坐 - Means 'but holds a sword and sits,' indicating a state of readiness or vigilance.
  • 忽有一物上阶推门门不开于狗窦中出头气休休然 - Describes a sudden appearance of an unknown creature that climbs the stairs, pushes the door, fails to open it, and then appears through a dog hole, breathing heavily.
  • 此人大怕把劔前斫不觉自倒劔失手抛落 - Describes the person's fear and the subsequent action of swinging the sword forward, losing balance, and dropping the sword.
  • 又不敢覔劔恐此物入来牀下跧伏更不敢动 - Describes the person's fear of searching for the sword, fearing the creature might enter under the bed and hide.
  • 忽然困睡不觉天明诸奴客已开关至閤子间但见狗窦中血淋漓狼籍众大惊呼儒士方悟开门尚自战栗具说昨宵与物战争之状众大骇异遂于此壁下寻唯见席防半破在地即夜所斫之鸟也乃故防破为风所吹如鸟动翼耳劔在狗窦侧众又绕堂寻血踪乃是所乗驴已斫口喙唇齿缺破乃是向晓因解头入狗门遂遭一劔众大笑絶倒扶持而归士人惊悸旬日方愈 - Describes the person's sudden sleep, the discovery of blood and the creature's remains, the realization of what happened, and the aftermath.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 劔 - Refers to a sword, a common weapon in ancient China.
  • 狗窦 - Refers to a small opening or hole, often used for ventilation or entry by small animals.
  • 儒士 - Refers to a scholar or learned person, often associated with Confucianism.
  • 驴 - Refers to a donkey, a common animal in ancient China.
  • 原化记 - Refers to a historical or literary work, possibly a collection of stories or anecdotes.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The phrase '有声役寝无音响恐惧既甚亦不敢寻究' can be interpreted as a compound sentence where the person hears sounds but does not hear any clear noise, causing great fear and preventing them from investigating further.
  • The phrase '忽有一物上阶推门门不开于狗窦中出头气休休然' can be read as a sequence of actions where an unknown creature climbs the stairs, pushes the door, fails to open it, and then appears through a dog hole, breathing heavily.
  • The phrase '此人大怕把劔前斫不觉自倒劔失手抛落' can be read as a sequence of actions where the person, in fear, swings the sword forward, loses balance, and drops the sword.
  • The phrase '又不敢覔劔恐此物入来牀下跧伏更不敢动' can be read as a sequence of actions where the person, in fear, does not search for the sword, fearing the creature might enter under the bed and hide.
  • The long sequence '忽然困睡不觉天明诸奴客已开关至閤子间但见狗窦中血淋漓狼籍众大惊呼儒士方悟开门尚自战栗具说昨宵与物战争之状众大骇异遂于此壁下寻唯见席防半破在地即夜所斫之鸟也乃故防破为风所吹如鸟动翼耳劔在狗窦侧众又绕堂寻血踪乃是所乗驴已斫口喙唇齿缺破乃是向晓因解头入狗门遂遭一劔众大笑絶倒扶持而归士人惊悸旬日方愈' can be read as a series of events where the person falls asleep, the servants and guests discover blood and the creature's remains, the person realizes what happened, and the aftermath.

Proper Nouns

  • 原化记 - A historical or literary work, possibly a collection of stories or anecdotes.

[5857]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 善網狐狢 - Skilled in trapping foxes and badgers; indicates proficiency in hunting or trapping.
  • 百无一失 - Without a single failure; infallible; emphasizes exceptional skill.
  • 偶乗暇 - Occasionally taking a break; when he had some spare time; indicates leisure or downtime.
  • 持矟 - Holding a spear; metaphor for carrying or wielding something.
  • 行旷野 - Walking in the wilderness; roaming the open fields; indicates a solitary journey.
  • 防日将夕 - As the sun was about to set; indicates the time of day.
  • 道左数百歩 - Hundreds of paces to the left of the road; indicates a specific location.
  • 冡岿然 - A mound standing tall; an imposing mound; indicates a noticeable burial site.
  • 草间细迳 - A narrow path through the grass; indicates a hidden or overgrown trail.
  • 若有人迹 - As if there were human footprints; indicates signs of human activity.
  • 遂入之 - He then entered it; indicates a decision to proceed.
  • 搅之 - He stirred it; he disturbed it; indicates an action in the dark.
  • 若有人捉拽之不得动 - As if someone was grabbing and pulling him but couldn't move him; indicates resistance.
  • 问尔人耶怪耶魅耶 - Are you a person, a ghost, or a demon?; indicates a questioning of the entity's nature.
  • 何故执吾矟而不置 - Why are you holding my spear and not letting go?; indicates a demand for explanation.
  • 闇中应曰 - The person in the dark replied; indicates a response.
  • 吾人也 - I am a person; indicates the entity's identity.
  • 乃命出之 - He then ordered him to come out; indicates a command.
  • 具以诚告 - He told him in full honesty; indicates a truthful account.
  • 我姓李 - My surname is Li; indicates the person's name.
  • 昨为盗系兖州军狱五木备体 - I was a bandit and was imprisoned in the army of Yanzhou, suffering from the five punishments; indicates a history of criminal activity and punishment.
  • 因伺隙逾狱垣亡命之 - I took advantage of an opportunity to escape over the prison wall and fled; indicates an escape.
  • 此死生唯命焉 - My life or death is now at the mercy of fate; indicates a sense of resignation.
  • 孟哀而将归 - Meng felt pity and decided to take him back; indicates an act of compassion.
  • 置于壁中 - He placed him in a niche in the wall; indicates a place of concealment.
  • 后经赦乃出 - Later, when he was pardoned, he was released; indicates a legal outcome.
  • 孟氏以善猎知名 - Meng was known for his skill in hunting; indicates a reputation.
  • 飞走之属无得脱者 - No flying or running animals could escape from him; indicates exceptional hunting ability.
  • 一旦塜之中而得叛狱囚以归 - One day, he found a prisoner who had escaped from prison in a tomb and took him back; indicates an unusual event.
  • 闻者皆大笑之 - Those who heard about it all laughed at him; indicates a reaction to the story.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 孟乙 - A personal name, likely referring to a specific individual.
  • 狐狢 - Refers to foxes and badgers, animals commonly hunted or trapped.
  • 百无一失 - A common idiom used to describe infallibility or exceptional skill.
  • 偶乗暇 - A phrase indicating leisure or downtime, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 持矟 - A metaphor for carrying or wielding something, often used in classical texts.
  • 行旷野 - A phrase indicating a solitary journey, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 防日将夕 - A phrase indicating the time of day, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 道左数百歩 - A phrase indicating a specific location, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 冡岿然 - A phrase indicating an imposing mound, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 草间细迳 - A phrase indicating a hidden or overgrown trail, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 若有人迹 - A phrase indicating signs of human activity, common in classical Chinese literature.

  • 闇中应曰 - A phrase indicating a response, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 吾人也 - A phrase indicating the entity's identity, common in classical Chinese literature.

  • 因伺隙逾狱垣亡命之 - A phrase indicating an escape, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 此死生唯命焉 - A phrase indicating a sense of resignation, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 孟哀而将归 - A phrase indicating an act of compassion, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 置于壁中 - A phrase indicating a place of concealment, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 后经赦乃出 - A phrase indicating a legal outcome, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 孟氏以善猎知名 - A phrase indicating a reputation, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 飞走之属无得脱者 - A phrase indicating exceptional hunting ability, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 一旦塜之中而得叛狱囚以归 - A phrase indicating an unusual event, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 闻者皆大笑之 - A phrase indicating a reaction to the story, common in classical Chinese literature.
  • 【出玉堂闲话】 - Indicates the source of the story, 'Yutang Xianhua' (闲话 from the Jade Hall), a collection of anecdotes or tales.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 孟乙 - A personal name, likely referring to a specific individual.
  • 善網狐狢 - Skilled in trapping foxes and badgers; indicates proficiency in hunting or trapping.
  • 百无一失 - Without a single failure; infallible; emphasizes exceptional skill.
  • 偶乗暇 - Occasionally taking a break; when he had some spare time; indicates leisure or downtime.
  • 持矟 - Holding a spear; metaphor for carrying or wielding something.
  • 行旷野 - Walking in the wilderness; roaming the open fields; indicates a solitary journey.
  • 防日将夕 - As the sun was about to set; indicates the time of day.
  • 道左数百歩 - Hundreds of paces to the left of the road; indicates a specific location.
  • 冡岿然 - A mound standing tall; an imposing mound; indicates a noticeable burial site.
  • 草间细迳 - A narrow path through the grass; indicates a hidden or overgrown trail.
  • 若有人迹 - As if there were human footprints; indicates signs of human activity.
  • 遂入之 - He then entered it; indicates a decision to proceed.
  • 搅之 - He stirred it; he disturbed it; indicates an action in the dark.
  • 若有人捉拽之不得动 - As if someone was grabbing and pulling him but couldn't move him; indicates resistance.
  • 问尔人耶怪耶魅耶 - Are you a person, a ghost, or a demon?; indicates a questioning of the entity's nature.
  • 何故执吾矟而不置 - Why are you holding my spear and not letting go?; indicates a demand for explanation.
  • 闇中应曰 - The person in the dark replied; indicates a response.
  • 吾人也 - I am a person; indicates the entity's identity.
  • 乃命出之 - He then ordered him to come out; indicates a command.
  • 具以诚告 - He told him in full honesty; indicates a truthful account.
  • 我姓李 - My surname is Li; indicates the person's name.
  • 昨为盗系兖州军狱五木备体 - I was a bandit and was imprisoned in the army of Yanzhou, suffering from the five punishments; indicates a history of criminal activity and punishment.
  • 因伺隙逾狱垣亡命之 - I took advantage of an opportunity to escape over the prison wall and fled; indicates an escape.
  • 此死生唯命焉 - My life or death is now at the mercy of fate; indicates a sense of resignation.
  • 孟哀而将归 - Meng felt pity and decided to take him back; indicates an act of compassion.
  • 置于壁中 - He placed him in a niche in the wall; indicates a place of concealment.
  • 后经赦乃出 - Later, when he was pardoned, he was released; indicates a legal outcome.
  • 孟氏以善猎知名 - Meng was known for his skill in hunting; indicates a reputation.
  • 飞走之属无得脱者 - No flying or running animals could escape from him; indicates exceptional hunting ability.
  • 一旦塜之中而得叛狱囚以归 - One day, he found a prisoner who had escaped from prison in a tomb and took him back; indicates an unusual event.
  • 闻者皆大笑之 - Those who heard about it all laughed at him; indicates a reaction to the story.

[5858]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 振武角觝人 - Literally 'people of Zhenwu who wrestle,' where '角觝' refers to a type of wrestling or combat performance.
  • 光啓年中 - Refers to the year during the Guangqi era, a period in Chinese history.
  • 置宴乐戏 - Means 'to set up a banquet with entertainment,' where '乐戏' refers to performances or shows.
  • 较力 - Means 'to compete in strength' or 'to test one's strength.'
  • 魁岸 - Describes someone as tall and strong.
  • 遂选三人相次而敌之 - Means 'then selected three people to challenge him one after another.'
  • 掩绾衣服 - Means 'to pull up one's clothes,' often used in the context of preparing for a fight.
  • 握左拳而前 - Means 'to grip the left fist and move forward.'
  • 懵然而倒 - Means 'to fall unconscious' or 'to collapse.'
  • 盥手而登席焉 - Means 'to wash hands and return to the seat.'
  • 怕酱见之辄倒 - Means 'afraid of seeing sauce and would fall over.'
  • 设厨求得少酱 - Means 'to prepare the kitchen and obtain a little sauce.'
  • 玉堂闲话 - A collection of anecdotes or stories, often used as a source for historical or cultural information.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 振武 - A military town or garrison, likely referring to a specific location in Chinese history.
  • 光啓 - The era name of Emperor Zhaozong of the Tang Dynasty (888–904).
  • 左神防军 - A military unit during the Tang Dynasty, possibly a specialized force.
  • 角觝 - A form of wrestling or combat performance that was popular in ancient China, often performed as part of entertainment at banquets or festivals.
  • 边岫判官 - A historical figure, likely a judge or official who documented or witnessed the event.
  • 玉堂闲话 - A collection of anecdotes or stories, often used as a source for historical or cultural information, similar to a modern-day encyclopedia of historical events.

Proper Nouns

  • 光啓 - The era name of Emperor Zhaozong of the Tang Dynasty.
  • 王卞 - A high-ranking military officer.
  • 振武 - A military town or garrison.
  • 边岫判官 - A judge named Bian Xu.
  • 玉堂闲话 - A collection of anecdotes or stories.

[5859]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 趙崇凝重清介 - Describes Zhao Chong as solemn, upright, and detached, emphasizing his moral integrity.
  • 門無雜賔 - Literally 'no mixed guests at the door,' meaning his household or social circle was composed of only pure and virtuous people.
  • 慕王蒙劉真長之風也 - Expresses admiration for the styles or virtues of Wang Meng and Liu Zhenzhang, two historical figures known for their integrity and wisdom.
  • 標格清峻 - Describes Zhao Chong as having a clear and lofty character or appearance.
  • 不為文章 - Means he did not engage in literary composition, possibly indicating a disdain for literary pursuits.
  • 號曰無字碑 - Literally 'called the 'wordless stele,' a nickname given to Zhao Chong, likely due to his lack of written works or his refusal to inscribe his achievements.
  • 每遇轉官舊例各舉一人自代 - Refers to the custom of recommending a successor when one’s position was to be filled, but Zhao Chong never did so.
  • 朝中無可代己者 - Means there was no one in the court worthy enough to replace him, which was used as a criticism against him.
  • 韓偓天復初入翰林 - Indicates that Han Wo entered the Hanlin Academy during the Tianfu era, marking the beginning of his official career.
  • 扈從之功 - Refers to his service accompanying the emperor, which was considered a significant achievement.
  • 返正初帝面許用偓為相 - Means the emperor initially promised to appoint Han Wo as chancellor upon returning to the capital.
  • 座主右仆射趙崇可以副陛下是選 - Suggests that Zhao Chong, as the examiner (座主) and Right Chancellor, would be suitable for the position.
  • 乞回臣之命授崇天下幸甚 - Means Han Wo requested that the emperor revoke his own appointment and instead appoint Zhao Chong, which would be fortunate for the empire.
  • 帝甚嘉叹 - Indicates the emperor highly praised and commended Han Wo’s suggestion.
  • 梁太祖在京素闻崇轻佻赞又有嫌衅 - Indicates that Liang Taizu (Liu Xiu) in the capital had heard of Zhao Chong’s frivolous nature and that there were already grievances against Wang Zan.
  • 驰入请见于帝前具言二公长短 - Means Liang Taizu rushed to the emperor’s presence to present his views on the strengths and weaknesses of Zhao Chong and Wang Zan.
  • 陛下运契中兴当须用重德镇风俗 - Suggests that during the era of restoration, it is necessary to use virtuous and respected individuals to stabilize the customs.
  • 手风慵展八行书眼病休看九局棋 - Describes Han Wo’s physical ailments, where his hand trembles so much he cannot write, and his eyes are so weak he cannot play chess.

[5860]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 迂僻 - Compound term meaning 'eccentric' or 'reclusive,' often used to describe someone who is aloof or difficult to approach.
  • 禀自天性 - 'Innate by nature,' indicating that the person's eccentricity is not learned but inherent.
  • 飞文染翰 - Classical idiom meaning 'to compose or write fluently,' referring to the ability to write or compose literary works.
  • 不可得之矣 - 'Cannot be attained,' indicating that the person is unable to write or compose effectively.
  • 礼容 - Refers to formal or respectful behavior, especially in the context of social etiquette.
  • 秉烛造室 - Classical expression meaning 'to enter the room with a candle,' symbolizing a formal or respectful visit.
  • 高谈虚论 - 'Engaging in lofty and abstract discussions,' often used to describe philosophical or literary discourse.
  • 茶果而退 - 'To have tea and fruit and then retire,' indicating the conclusion of a formal meeting or visit.
  • 帏房 - Refers to the inner chamber or private quarters, often used in the context of conjugal or intimate relations.
  • 卜其嘉防 - Phrase meaning 'to select a good time for marriage,' referencing the traditional Chinese practice of choosing auspicious days for important events.
  • 请而可之 - 'To request and receive approval,' indicating the formal process of seeking permission.
  • 潜师宵遁 - 'To secretly move the army at night and retreat,' a military term for a covert withdrawal.
  • 掩袭 - 'To attack unexpectedly' or 'to ambush.'
  • 攀鞍 - 'To grasp the saddle,' indicating a moment of hesitation or remembrance while mounted.
  • 传语书记速请上马 - 'Tell the secretary to hurry and get on the horse,' indicating an urgent command.
  • 藏身 - 'To hide oneself,' used here in the context of evading or avoiding.
  • 忌日礼不见客 - 'On the day of mourning, the ritual does not receive guests,' indicating that on a day of mourning or memorial, one does not entertain visitors.
  • 人妖 - Term that can mean 'a human demon' or 'an abnormal person,' suggesting that the person is considered strange or unnatural.
  • 秦陇人皆知之 - 'All the people of Qin and Long knew this,' indicating widespread knowledge of the person's behavior.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 岐王李茂贞 - Li Maowen, a powerful warlord during the late Tang dynasty, who controlled the regions of Qin (Qinzhong, modern Shaanxi) and Long (Longyou, modern Gansu). He was known for his military power and political influence.
  • 泾州 - A state or region in the northwest of China, historically part of the Tang dynasty's frontier territories, located in present-day Gansu.
  • 书记 - A title for an official or scribe, often responsible for writing, record-keeping, or correspondence in a military or administrative setting.
  • 天水 - A city in Gansu province, historically a strategic location in the region controlled by Li Maowen.
  • 青泥岭 - A mountain pass in the Qinling Mountains, historically a key location in military campaigns between the Tang and the Western Shu (Sichuan) forces.
  • 蜀人 - Refers to people from the Western Shu (Sichuan) region, a powerful state during the late Tang and early Five Dynasties period.
  • 梁人 - Refers to the Later Liang, one of the Five Dynasties that succeeded the Tang dynasty. This term may also refer to the Western Liang, depending on the historical context.
  • 宵遁 - A military term for a night retreat or secret withdrawal, often used in the context of a strategic or defensive maneuver.
  • 忌日 - A day of mourning or remembrance, often associated with the death of a family member or important figure. In this context, it may refer to a day of mourning for a deceased leader or patron.
  • 玉堂闲话 - The source of this passage, a classical Chinese collection of anecdotes and stories, likely from the Tang or Song dynasty. The title translates to 'Idle Talk from the Jade Hall' or 'Stories from the Hall of Jade.'

Proper Nouns

  • 薛昌绪 - Xue Changxu, the subject of the passage, a secretary or scribe under the command of Li Maowen.
  • 岐王李茂贞 - Li Maowen, also known as the Prince of Qi, a powerful warlord during the late Tang dynasty.
  • 泾州 - Jingzhou, a region in Gansu.
  • 泾帅 - The general in charge of Jingzhou.
  • 天水 - Tianshui, a city in Gansu.
  • 青泥岭 - Qingniling, a mountain pass in the Qinling Mountains.
  • 蜀人 - People of the Western Shu (Sichuan).
  • 梁人 - People of the Later Liang, one of the Five Dynasties.
  • 玉堂闲话 - Yutang Xianhua, a classical Chinese collection of anecdotes and stories, likely from the Tang or Song dynasty.

[5861]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 失其名 - Lost his name, meaning the individual's name is unknown or has been omitted.
  • 許田人也 - A man from Xutian, indicating the character's place of origin.
  • 幼年為黃巾所掠 - In childhood, captured by the Yellow Turban rebels, referring to a major rebel force during the late Eastern Han dynasty.
  • 亡失父母 - Lost his parents, indicating that the character lost his parents at a young age.
  • 從先主征伐 - Followed the Lord (likely Liu Bei) in military campaigns, highlighting the character's military service.
  • 後繼領數鎮節钺 - Succeeded in leading multiple regions with symbols of authority and military power.
  • 官至极品 - Reached the highest rank, indicating the character's pinnacle of career achievement.
  • 有掌廐夫姜老者 - An elderly man named Jiang who served as a stable master.
  • 事刍秣數十年 - Served in the stable for several decades, indicating long-term service.
  • 姜每入廐見其小過必笞之 - Whenever entering the stable, seeing small faults, he would always whip them, indicating strict discipline.
  • 如是積年計其數將及數百 - In this manner, over many years, counting the number, approaching several hundred, indicating frequent discipline.
  • 後老不任鞭棰因泣告夫人乞放歸鄉里 - Later, the old man could no longer endure the whip, so he wept and told his wife, begged to be allowed to return to his hometown.
  • 夫人曰汝何許人 - The lady asked where the old man was from.
  • 對曰許田人復有何骨肉 - He replied, a man from Xutian, do you have any family members.
  • 又問其兒小字及妻姓氏行第並房眷近親皆言之 - He also asked about his son's nickname, and his wife's surname and lineage, and told about his relatives and close family members.
  • 及姜歸宅夫人具言姜老欲乞假歸鄉 - When Jiang returned home, the lady told everything, the old man wanted to take leave and return to his hometown.
  • 因問得所失男女亲属姓名姜大驚疑其父也 - Upon asking, he learned the names of the lost family members, Jiang was greatly surprised and suspected it was his father.
  • 使人細問之其男身有何記驗曰我兒腳心上有一黑子余不記之 - He sent someone to ask in detail, what marks does his son have, he said my son has a black spot on his sole, but I don't remember anything else.
  • 姜大哭宻遣人送出劔門之外奏先主曰臣父近自关东来遂将金帛车马迎入宅父子如初 - Jiang cried bitterly, secretly sent someone to escort him out of the gate, reported to the Lord, my father recently came from the east, so I brought him back with gold, silk, and carriages, and we were reunited like before.
  • 報挞父之過齋僧數萬終身不挞從者 - To make amends for beating his father, to feed thousands of monks, never to beat his subordinates again.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 姜太師 - A high-ranking military official, likely a general or chancellor, during the late Eastern Han dynasty or the Three Kingdoms period.
  • 黃巾 - Refers to the Yellow Turban rebels, a major insurgent force during the late Eastern Han dynasty.

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 沈尚书 - Refers to a high-ranking official named Shen.
  • 狼戾而不谨 - Describes the wife as fierce and not careful.
  • 妬忌 - Means jealousy.
  • 如在狴牢之中 - Compares the husband's situation to being in prison.
  • 挈其妻孥寄于鳯州 - Means taking his wife and children to Fengzhou.
  • 徃东川游索 - Means going to Dongchuan to wander and seek.
  • 与怨偶永絶矣 - Means to permanently sever ties with his wife.
  • 华洪镇东蜀 - Refers to Huahong, a military governor of Dongchuan.
  • 有布衣之旧 - Means having an old acquaintance from civilian life.
  • 郊迎 - Means meeting at the outskirts of the city.
  • 执手叙其契濶 - Means shaking hands and recounting their long-standing friendship.
  • 待之如亲兄 - Means treating him like a close brother.
  • 特创一第 - Means building a special house.
  • 仆马金帛器玩 - Refers to servants, horses, gold, silk, and playthings.
  • 无有阙者 - Means nothing was lacking.

  • 无心还家 - Means having no intention of returning home.

  • 大惧 - Means greatly afraid.
  • 遣人却之 - Means sending someone to reject her.
  • 重设盟誓 - Means making a serious oath again.
  • 自此必改从前之性 - Means promising to change her previous nature.
  • 愿以偕老 - Means wishing to live together until old age.
  • 颇亦柔和 - Means somewhat gentle.
  • 前行复作 - Means resuming her previous behavior.
  • 诸姬婢仆悉鞭棰星散 - Means all the concubines, maids, and servants were beaten and scattered.
  • 头面皆拏擘破损 - Means his face and head were torn and injured.
  • 召沈谓之曰 - Means calling Shen and saying to him.
  • 欲为兄杀之如何 - Means wanting to kill her for you, what do you think?
  • 不可如是 - Means cannot be done like this.
  • 魂神失措 - Means losing one's spirit and composure.
  • 知怨偶为仇也 - Means knowing that the wife has become an enemy.
  • 悲哉沈之宿有仇乎 - Means it is sad that Shen had a grudge against his wife.

[5864]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 甞出入 - Means 'frequently entering and exiting' or 'often visiting,' indicating regular interaction.
  • 稍接从容 - Means 'gradually becoming acquainted' or 'getting to know someone over time.'
  • 形于言 - Means 'expressed in words' or 'made manifest through speech.'
  • 打人唇绽齿落 - A hyperbolic expression meaning 'to strike someone so hard that their lips split and teeth fall out,' used here to emphasize the difficulty of accessing something.
  • 岭外之地非贬不去 - Means 'the land beyond the mountains cannot be reached without being exiled,' a proverbial expression.
  • 假一第而处之 - Means 'borrowed a house to live in,' indicating temporary residence.
  • 闻多凶怪 - Means 'heard of many ominous and strange occurrences.'
  • 昏暝即不敢出戸庭 - Means 'at dusk, dared not leave the house,' indicating fear.
  • 合门惊惧 - Means 'the whole family was terrified,' indicating collective fear.
  • 莫能安寝 - Means 'could not sleep peacefully,' indicating restlessness.
  • 忽一夕闻吼声 - Means 'one night suddenly heard a roar,' indicating a sudden and alarming sound.
  • 若有呼于瓮中者 - Means 'as if someone was calling from inside a jar,' indicating a strange and confined sound.
  • 举家怖惧 - Means 'the whole family was terrified,' indicating collective fear.
  • 必谓其怪之尤者 - Means 'certainly thought it was the most extraordinary of the strange occurrences,' indicating a heightened sense of fear.
  • 月色晦 - Means 'the moonlight was dim,' indicating poor visibility.
  • 似若狗身而首不能举 - Means 'seemed like a dog with a head that could not be raised,' indicating a distorted and unusual appearance.
  • 以挝击其脑 - Means 'struck its head with a stick,' indicating a physical action.
  • 轰然一声 - Means 'a loud bang,' indicating a sudden and powerful sound.
